headline: BofA Sees AI Driving the Next $1 Trillion in Semiconductor Revenue
summary: Bank of America believes artificial intelligence will accelerate growth across the semiconductor industry, helping generate an additional $1 trillion in chip sales over the next five years and reshaping several key technology markets in the process. “The chip industry took ~50 years to generate its first $1Tn in sales,” analysts led by Vivek Arya wrote.

raw.headline: BofA Sees AI Driving the Next $1 Trillion in Semiconductor Revenue

headline: Once JPMorgan’s Top Trader, He’s Now a Favorite to Succeed Jamie Dimon as CEO
summary: Over two decades at JPMorgan, Troy Rohrbaugh earned a reputation as a shrewd risk manager. Now he’ll go from investing banking to running the bank’s sprawling consumer operation.

headline: Dollar Jumps 2.1% as Wall Street Banks Turn Bullish on Fed Outlook
summary: JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs and Bank of America see stronger dollar prospects amid resilient US growth and AI-driven capital inflows.

headline: Is AI momentum starting to cool? BofA weighs in
summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America warned that the artificial intelligence-driven momentum rally may be losing steam, flagging growing risks around monetization and commodification that could pressure corporate earnings and equity markets globally.

headline: Pacsun: 'Participatory' Gen Z, Gen Alpha driving consumer resilience
summary: Brieane Olson, CEO of Pacsun discusses consumer trends amongst the brand's younger audience, and how the inflationary environment is impacting the business.

headline: US Stocks Post First Outflow Since March as Tech Trade Falters
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Investors pulled money from US equities for the first time in three months, with record withdrawals from tech funds signaling that the artificial-intelligence trade is cooling.
